text: Project - 2x(500MW or above) Main Plant Package Section- 2 (Steam Generator and Auxiliaries) 2 - 3 Note: a) Pressure drop in reheater should not exceed 2.5 kg/cm 2 under all operating conditions. b) The steam temperatures at superheater and reheater outlet(s) shall be guaranteed to be maintained at +5 0 C of the rated value at all loads within control range, under all operating conditions including HP heaters out of service condition. 2.1.6 Fuels A) Coal i) The plant systems shall be designed considering project specific data 1 on coal quality and ash analysis. However, the typical co al quality parameters on as received basis are indicated below: - . Sl. No. Particulars Units Performance coal Worst coal A. Proximate analysis 1. Moisture % 10 15 2. Ash % 35 45 3. Fixed carbon % 31 20 4. Volatile matter % 24 20 Total % 100 100 B. Gross calorific value kcal/kg 4100 3200 C. Grindability index HGI 50 50 D. Ultimate analysis 1. Carbon % 43.02 32.88 2. Hydrogen % 2.82 2.13 3. Sulphur % 0.45 0.28 4. Nitrogen % 0.81 0.59 5. Oxygen (by difference) % 7.90 4.12 6. Moisture % 10.0 15.0 7. Ash % 35 45 Total % 100 100 1 Coal and ash analysis data to be furnished by the purchaser. Standard Technical Specification for Sub- critical Thermal Power Project - 2x(500MW or above) Main Plant Package Section- 2 (Steam Generator and Auxiliaries) 2 - 4 ii) Typical Ash analysis Characteristics Range/ value SiO2 54 to 60 % Al2O3 27 to 31 % Fe2O3 3 to 5% TiO2 0.3 to 0.5% CaO 1 to 3% MgO 0.5 to 1.5% Chloride as Cl 0.03 to 0.06% Sulphate as SO4 0.1 to 0.2% Alkalies as NaOH < 0.01mg/kg Chromium as Cr 25 to 28 mg/kg Mercury as Hg < 0.002 mg/kg Lead as Pb <0.1 mg/kg Cadmium as Cd < 0.01 mg/kg Nickel as Ni 71 to 95 mg/kg Zink as Zn 84 to 86 mg/kg Copper as Cu 56 to 60 mg/kg iii) Correction curves shall be furnished by the bidder for the variation of coal quality parameters for making adjustment on calculated efficiency during performance guarantee test of the unit(s). iv) The steam generator and its auxiliaries shall be capable of giving the guaranteed maximum continuous output under the most adverse conditions satisfactorily when firing coal as per the variations in the coal quality
